## [00:00 - 03:04]

Hey everybody, Sasha here from Pol's team and if you are curious or have been needing a tracker or curious how to track commissions, then this short training is definitely for you. So we've got a column for the client and then we have a column for the commission percentage. So this is fully obviously editable. Do you pay 7% do you pay 10% do you pay in a half percent and obviously this can be edited for each individual deal. So if there's a different amount paid per deal, you can edit it accordingly. Also the sale date. So you'll want to make sure you put the sale date in there and then I've also set up a column for the setter. So I want this for cross reference, right? So I would love the closer to tell me if there was a setter, if you have multiple setters, this is really important. If there even was a setter, maybe there's no setter. So having this to then cross reference to the setters tracker is really, really, really helpful. So I have a column there for a setter and then I also put a column here. If there's anybody else involved with this client, is there an account manager? Is there an accountability coach that's assigned? So there's a field here for you to note that as well. And then the total revenue generated. So this is the total deal amount as well as the payment terms. Was this a pay in full? Was this a to pay? Is this a for pay? Whatever your payment plans are, it's really helpful to note that here because then we can populate for each month going out. So I have an example here, John Smith, his sale date was 215K for a to pay. So because it's a to pay, right, we'd have in the month of February, 12.5 and then in the month of March, 12.5 or whatever the payments agreed upon is there. So you know, you'll see everything spread out on a per month basis. And I have a projected collection. So this is what's projected, right? We know we want to collect 12,500 in February from John and we know we want to collect or anticipate collecting 12.5 in March. So that way there's a projected commission for your closer.

## [03:04 - 06:07]

They can go in and see right across the top here what the projected amount and then what the actual amount is what I populate, right? As things come through Stripe or your payment processor, whomever you're using, I actually have this other tab set up where you can put in the data, the transaction data that's coming through as backup data. So you'd have the name, the date, the amount and whatever kind of transaction data confirmation number that you're getting from your payment processor. I kind of list each and every transaction here in this tab for this particular closer so that all the backup data is here. And then as those come through, I then go in and populate this gray column for actual collected. So we're reconciling it. We're reconciling each and every payment that comes in for each and every client so that way the commissions are accurate. Of course this template is based on cash collected. So there's you know you can edit this and have this personalized for your closer for your in-house processes but I have it just defaulted for 10% for the sale paid out on actual cash collected. And we have a minimum threshold. So it's a minimum. You know we kind of coach you guys to pay out for a minimum threshold and I just have it defaulted at you know 3K or 33%. If that's different, you know edit that right here for your closer so they know. But you know let's say you know we were projecting and anticipating 12.5 to come in but when I'm looking at the records, I'm populating in only 5K came in right. Only 5K as of yet. ## [06:07 - 08:17]

So this is important because the setters a little bit different. They get paid per close. You know I have it 300 per close or 3% up to 750. You of course edit this to be what's actual you know what you do in your company. I just threw an example in there and so basically it's paid out upon close but there's that threshold of 33% cash collected. So you're not going to want to pay it out. You know if this deal is 25,000 and yet you only collected 5,000 you're not going to want to pay it out until that threshold is met. So it's interesting to know if this is 25,000 and it's like a 5 pay you're going to want to track it you know 5,000 this month, 5,000 that month. You're going to want to track what actually came in so that it's paid out when you actually hit that threshold of the 33%. So hopefully that makes sense.
